title: RepoMind — Give Your Repository a Brain
subtitle: Ask your codebase anything. The answer is the path.
author: RepoMind

slides.title: RepoMind
slides.subtitle: Give your repository a brain

title.text: RepoMind — Give Your Repository a Brain

slides.notes: Hook: RepoMind turns any git repo into a typed knowledge graph and answers questions by walking the relationships. Explainable by design. Powered by Cognee.

slides.title: Your Repo Knows. You Can't Ask It.

title.text: Your Repo Knows. You Can't Ask It.

bodyPrimary.text: "Why was auth built this way, and who decided it?" The answer is buried across commits, closed PRs, and stale issues. Keyword search can't connect the dots.

slides.notes: The pain, in one line: institutional knowledge is trapped in git history. Plain vector RAG retrieves similar text and hopes the answer is inside.

slides.title: We Mapped the Entire Cognee Repo

title.text: We Mapped the Entire Cognee Repo

slides.notes: Impact-first proof: RepoMind ran on the real Cognee open-source repo end to end — every commit, PR, and issue, plus 936 people and 4,815 files, 128,899 typed edges.

slides.title: Not Just Search — Traversal

bodySecondary.text: Returns textually similar chunks and hopes the answer is inside.

body-4.text: Walks typed relationships. The traversal path IS the explainable citation.

slides.notes: The killer differentiator judges should remember: file to commit to PR to reviewer to issue. Every answer comes with an auditable path.

slides.title: One Idempotent Pipeline, Three Layers

bodySecondary.text: Git, GitHub, Slack, Discord — one Source interface.

body-4.text: One path: upsert, resolve refs, park/retry, tombstone.

body-20.text: Typed KB + Cognee hybrid graph+vector recall.

slides.notes: Every trigger — backfill, webhook, poller, manual sync — funnels through the same idempotent ingestor. Structure stays exact and local; recall is delegated to Cognee.

slides.title: Correctness-First By Design

body-20.text: Numeric refs never guessed — parked till resolved.

slides.notes: Accuracy over speed. Every guarantee is tested offline against the in-memory store — no network, no keys required.

slides.title: Pluggable Memory: Pick Your Backend

bodySecondary.text: Offline, deterministic, zero AI deps — ideal for CI.

body-4.text: Local AI: Ollama + embedded kuzu, fully offline.

body-20.text: Hosted AI — just a key + URL, nothing to self-host.

slides.notes: Same typed structural graph across all three, so behavior and tests transfer directly. Swap backends with one config line.

slides.title: See It In Action
slides.notes: Transition into the demo and product.

slides.title: What Makes It Shine

items: Multi-hop answers: file -> commit -> PR -> reviewer -> issue
Chat-to-code bridge: #123 links messages to PRs and issues
GitHub-themed UI: structured reports + graph zoom & fullscreen
Secure by default: scoped API keys, per-key rate limits

slides.notes: The UI renders an entity-highlighted answer report with grounded-on sources and a live force-graph of the traversal path.

slides.title: Live Demo

bodyPrimary.text: Ask a question -> get a structured, cited answer -> watch the traversal light up as a live graph. Zoom in, go fullscreen, explore the 18,890-node Cognee brain.

slides.notes: DEMO CUE: Open the UI, paste the admin key, ask 'who reviewed pull requests about the graph store'. Show the report card, the seed sources, the numbered traversal, then the fullscreen graph.

slides.title: Built to a High Bar
slides.notes: Fully tested offline with a throwaway git repo and fake GitHub objects. Dependency-inverted so every path is unit-testable without network or keys.

slides.title: What's Next

items: Richer query engine: natural-language planning over the graph
More connectors: Jira, Linear, Confluence, CI logs
Incremental live sync at scale with parallel fetching
Team dashboards: ownership, review load, decision history

slides.notes: Vision slide for judges: RepoMind is Phase 1+2 today; the roadmap turns it into a living memory for any engineering org.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.notes: Recap: typed knowledge graph, explainable multi-hop answers, correctness-first ingestion, pluggable Cognee memory. Open floor for questions.
